title: Two-Armed Bandit
description: A classic decision-making task from cognitive science.
instructions:
You'll see two buttons (slot machine arms). Each one has a hidden probability of giving you a reward. Your goal is to earn as many points as possible over 80 trials.

On each trial, choose one arm by clicking it or pressing 1/2. After each choice, you'll see whether you won a point.

The key challenge: one arm pays out more often than the other, but you need to figure out which one through trial and error.
debrief: This task measures your exploration-exploitation trade-off. The Rescorla-Wagner model estimates learning rate (alpha) and inverse temperature (tau) from your choices.
